sources.notes: TCGA Ovarian Cancer 2011 Nature. This paper had the most intact full text in PMC. Core SMGs (9 total): TP53 (303/316 = 96% stated explicitly), BRCA1 (germline 9% + somatic 3%), BRCA2 (germline 8% + somatic 3%), NF1, RB1, CDK12, CSMD3, FAT3, GABRA6. CSMD3/FAT3/GABRA6 explicitly noted in text as 'not appearing to be expressed in HGS-OvCa' so excluded from comprehensive list. Rare driver mutations named from COSMIC matching: PIK3CA (E545K, H1047R), KRAS (G12D), and Q61R (gene name stripped — NRAS based on COSMIC/ovarian cancer biology context). Copy number amplification peaks named with frequencies: CCNE1 (>20%, mutually exclusive with BRCA), MYC (>20%), MECOM/3q26.2 (Immunoreactive subtype), TERT, PRKCI/CDT1/ID4 (focal peaks). HR deficiency pathway named genes: BRCA1 (11% methylation silencing), BRIP1, RAD51C, PALB2, Fanconi Anemia genes (5%). FOXM1 named as transcription factor network hub in 87% of cases with target genes CCNE2 and BIRC5. NOTCH pathway altered 23% (NOTCH3 named). RB1 pathway deregulated 67%, PI3K/RAS 45%. Discussion names other OvCa subtype-specific genes to contrast HGS: clear-cell=ARID1A+PTEN, endometrioid=CTNNB1+APC+ARID1A+PIK3CA+PTEN, mucinous=KRAS. These are named with data (mutation rates) even though they are contrasting subtypes. BIRC5/Survivin and CCNE2 included as FOXM1 targets named with expression evidence. Excluded: chemokine receptor genes (CCL2, CXCR3) that define expression subtypes but are not cancer drivers; HOX genes (generic subtype marker without specific results); survival signature genes (193-gene list not individually named in text).
